description: Search for flights across multiple providers

args.from: Origin airport IATA code(s), comma-separated for multi-airport cities (e.g. EZE, JFK,EWR,LGA)
args.to: Destination airport IATA code(s), comma-separated for multi-airport cities (e.g. JFK, CDG, MAD)
args.return: Return date YYYY-MM-DD (OPTIONAL — omit this key entirely for one-way flights)

routerHints: For IATA codes: use comma-separated codes for multi-airport cities: Buenos Aires → EZE,AEP. New York → JFK,EWR,LGA. London → LHR,LGW,STN. Paris → CDG,ORY. Milan → MXP,LIN. São Paulo → GRU,CGH. Rio → GIG,SDU. Chicago → ORD,MDW. Washington → IAD,DCA. For single-airport cities use just one code (e.g. Madrid → MAD). Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD. If no year given, use the current or next occurrence. dd/mm format (Spanish/Argentine): day/month not month/day. If NO return date is mentioned, omit the 'return' arg entirely (one-way search). Only include 'return' if the user explicitly mentions a return date.
